best_individual.algorithm: The algorithm computes a composite priority as the weighted normalized slack time (due date minus current time) divided by number of operations, adjusted by machine utilization and processing urgency, with smaller values indicating higher priority.

The new algorithm computes priority by using effective slack (due date minus the maximum of task start time and machine available time) normalized by number of operations, then adds dynamic utilization penalty (scaled up if machine is busy relative to task start) and processing time ratio penalty, minus urgency factor, with smaller values indicating higher priority.

The new algorithm computes priority by using effective slack (due date minus the maximum of task start time and machine available time) normalized by number of operations, then adds dynamic utilization penalty (scaled up if machine is busy relative to task start) and processing time ratio penalty, minus urgency factor, with smaller values indicating higher priority.
New algorithm uses weighted effective slack minus weighted processing time urgency, plus normalized utilization penalty and machine load factor, with smaller values indicating higher priority.
New algorithm uses a weighted combination of relative slack, machine congestion, and processing urgency with different coefficients to compute priority, where smaller values indicate higher priority.
Synthesize a weighted priority combining normalized slack with machine congestion and urgency, using adaptive coefficients and safeguard checks.
The new algorithm uses a quadratic penalty for slack combined with a machine starvation index and a processing time deviation factor to compute priority.

The new algorithm combines a quadratic slack penalty from algorithm 1 with a normalized slack and dynamic weighting from algorithm 2, using a composite priority that balances urgency, congestion, machine fit, and eligibility to minimize tardiness.

The new algorithm enhances the quadratic slack penalty with normalized slack, machine starvation index, dynamic machine fit, availability factor, and eligibility boost, using adaptive weighting that penalizes tight slack more aggressively while incorporating congestion and urgency to minimize tardiness.

The new algorithm enhances priority calculation by adding a machine-task capability score, incorporating a start-time delay penalty, and adjusting the starvation index for machine type eligibility, all combined with adaptive weighting to minimize tardiness.
